description: Add ReadFileRequest and WriteFileRequest types to Rust backend
steps: Open src-tauri/src/filesystem/types.rs
Add ReadFileRequest struct with path: String field
Add WriteFileRequest struct with path and content: String fields
Derive Debug and Deserialize for both structs

description: Write failing tests for read_file command in Rust
steps: Open src-tauri/src/filesystem/commands.rs test module
Add test: read_file_returns_content (reads file from temp dir)
Add test: read_file_rejects_path_outside_home (security check)
Add test: read_file_rejects_directory (validates file type)
Run cargo test to verify failures

description: Implement read_file Tauri command with home-directory scoping
steps: Add read_file function to src-tauri/src/filesystem/commands.rs
Expand ~ to home directory using expand_home helper
Canonicalize path and verify it's within home directory
Verify path is a file (not directory)
Read file content as UTF-8 string
Run cargo test to verify all read_file tests pass

description: Write failing tests for write_file command in Rust
steps: Open src-tauri/src/filesystem/commands.rs test module
Add test: write_file_creates_file (writes content to new file)
Add test: write_file_creates_parent_dirs (creates missing directories)
Add test: write_file_rejects_path_outside_home (security check)
Add test: write_file_overwrites_existing (updates existing file)
Run cargo test to verify failures

description: Implement write_file Tauri command with home-directory scoping
steps: Add write_file function to src-tauri/src/filesystem/commands.rs
Expand ~ to home directory
Verify path is within home directory before write
Create parent directories if needed (fs::create_dir_all)
Write content to file (overwrites existing)
Run cargo test to verify all write_file tests pass

description: Register read_file and write_file in Tauri invoke handler
steps: Export read_file and write_file from src-tauri/src/filesystem/mod.rs
Import in src-tauri/src/lib.rs (add to use filesystem:: statement)
Add read_file and write_file to invoke_handler! macro
Run cargo build to verify compilation
Run cargo clippy to ensure no warnings

description: Install CodeMirror 6 and vim extension packages
steps: Run npm install codemirror @codemirror/view @codemirror/state @codemirror/language
Run npm install @codemirror/lang-javascript @codemirror/lang-rust @codemirror/lang-json
Run npm install @codemirror/lang-css @codemirror/lang-html
Run npm install @replit/codemirror-vim
Verify package-lock.json updates
Run npm install to verify clean install

description: Extend IFileSystemService with readFile and writeFile methods
steps: Open src/features/files/services/fileSystemService.ts
Add readFile(path: string): Promise<string> to interface
Add writeFile(path: string, content: string): Promise<void> to interface
Implement readFile in TauriFileSystemService using invoke('read_file')
Implement writeFile in TauriFileSystemService using invoke('write_file')
Update MockFileSystemService with stub implementations
Write unit tests in fileSystemService.test.ts

description: Create language service for filename to CodeMirror language mapping
steps: Create src/features/editor/services/languageService.ts
Implement getLanguageExtension(filename: string) function
Map .ts/.tsx to typescript(), .rs to rust(), .json to json(), etc.
Default to null for unknown extensions
Create src/features/editor/services/languageService.test.ts
Write tests for each language mapping (.ts, .rs, .json, .css, .html)
Run npm test to verify

description: Create Catppuccin Mocha CodeMirror theme matching Obsidian Lens
steps: Create src/features/editor/theme/catppuccin.ts
Define EditorView theme with Catppuccin Mocha colors
Map editor background to #121221 (surface)
Map gutter background to #1a1a2a (surface-container-low)
Map cursor to #e2c7ff (primary)
Define syntax highlighting: keywords mauve, strings green, functions blue
Create src/features/editor/theme/catppuccin.test.ts
Write structure validation tests

description: Create useCodeMirror hook to manage EditorView instance
steps: Create src/features/editor/hooks/useCodeMirror.ts
Accept containerRef, initialContent, language, onSave callback
Initialize EditorView with vim(), catppuccin theme, language extension
Configure vim :w command to call onSave callback
Return editorView instance and updateContent function
Clean up EditorView on unmount
Create src/features/editor/hooks/useCodeMirror.test.ts
Write tests for initialization, vim config, cleanup

description: Create useVimMode hook to track vim mode from CodeMirror
steps: Create src/features/editor/hooks/useVimMode.ts
Accept editorView instance as parameter
Listen to vim mode change events from @replit/codemirror-vim
Track current mode: NORMAL, INSERT, VISUAL, COMMAND
Return current vim mode string
Create src/features/editor/hooks/useVimMode.test.ts
Write tests for mode tracking

description: Create useEditorBuffer hook for file state management
steps: Create src/features/editor/hooks/useEditorBuffer.ts
Track filePath, originalContent, isDirty state
Implement openFile(path) function using fileSystemService.readFile
Implement saveFile() function using fileSystemService.writeFile
Implement dirty detection by comparing current vs original content
Return file state, openFile, saveFile, isDirty
Create src/features/editor/hooks/useEditorBuffer.test.ts
Write tests for open, save, dirty detection

description: Replace Shiki with CodeMirror in CodeEditor component
steps: Open src/features/editor/components/CodeEditor.tsx
Remove Shiki HTML rendering and shikiService import
Add container div ref for CodeMirror mount
Use useCodeMirror hook with file content and language
Use useVimMode hook to get current mode
Use useEditorBuffer hook for file management
Wire vim :w command to save file via useEditorBuffer
Update src/features/editor/components/CodeEditor.test.tsx
Test CodeMirror renders, vim mode tracking, save functionality

description: Create UnsavedChangesDialog component with glassmorphism style
steps: Create src/features/editor/components/UnsavedChangesDialog.tsx
Accept isOpen, fileName, onSave, onDiscard, onCancel props
Render backdrop with backdrop-blur-sm bg-black/40
Render glassmorphism panel matching command palette style
Add three buttons: Save (primary), Discard (error), Cancel (neutral)
Use framer-motion for spring animation (stiffness 400, damping 30)
Handle Esc key to call onCancel
Create src/features/editor/components/UnsavedChangesDialog.test.tsx
Test render, button callbacks, keyboard handling

description: Wire VimStatusBar to real CodeMirror vim state
steps: Open src/features/editor/components/VimStatusBar.tsx
Accept vimMode and isDirty props
Display vimMode (NORMAL/INSERT/VISUAL/COMMAND)
Display [+] indicator when isDirty is true
Update layout to match design spec
Update src/features/editor/components/VimStatusBar.test.tsx
Test mode display and dirty indicator

description: Update BottomDrawer to be resizable and receive file selection
steps: Open src/features/workspace/components/BottomDrawer.tsx
Add useResizable hook with vertical axis
Set default height to 50% of parent
Set min height 150px, max 80%
Add drag handle at top edge
Accept selectedFilePath prop and pass to CodeEditor
Update BottomDrawer.test.tsx for resize functionality

description: Wire FileExplorer onFileSelect to parent WorkspaceView
steps: Open src/features/workspace/components/panels/FileExplorer.tsx
Add onFileSelect callback prop
Call onFileSelect(filePath) when file is clicked
Update FileExplorer.test.tsx to verify callback

description: Update WorkspaceView with file selection state and unsaved changes flow
steps: Open src/features/workspace/WorkspaceView.tsx
Add selectedFilePath state
Add showUnsavedDialog state
Implement handleFileSelect: check isDirty, show dialog if needed
Pass selectedFilePath to BottomDrawer
Render UnsavedChangesDialog with save/discard/cancel handlers
Update grid template columns (sidebar 340px, activity 360px)
Update WorkspaceView.test.tsx for file selection flow

description: Update Sidebar default sizes for better visibility
steps: Open src/features/workspace/components/Sidebar.tsx
Update default width from 256px to 340px
Update max width from 480px to 560px
Update FileExplorer default height from 240px to 320px
Verify resize handles still work correctly
Update Sidebar.test.tsx if needed

description: Write integration test for file open flow
steps: Create integration test file or add to existing suite
Test: FileExplorer click → read_file → CodeMirror content update
Verify file path state updates
Verify editor content matches file content
Verify isDirty starts as false
Run npm test to verify

description: Write integration test for unsaved changes flow
steps: Test: Open file → edit content → isDirty becomes true
Test: Click different file → UnsavedChangesDialog shows
Test: Click Save → write_file called → new file opens
Test: Click Discard → new file opens without saving
Test: Click Cancel → stays on current file
Run npm test to verify

description: Write integration test for vim save command
steps: Test: Open file → edit content → type :w in vim
Verify write_file Tauri command is invoked
Verify isDirty becomes false after save
Verify VimStatusBar removes [+] indicator
Run npm test to verify

description: Remove Shiki-related files and dependencies
steps: Remove src/features/editor/services/shikiService.ts
Remove src/features/editor/services/shikiService.test.ts
Remove src/features/editor/components/LineNumbers.tsx (if exists)
Remove shiki from package.json dependencies
Run npm install to clean lock file
Search codebase for remaining shiki imports

description: Run all quality checks and fix issues
steps: Run npm run lint and fix all ESLint issues
Run npm run type-check and fix TypeScript errors
Run npm test and achieve 80%+ coverage
Run cd src-tauri && cargo clippy and fix warnings
Run cd src-tauri && cargo test and verify all pass
Run npm run format to apply Prettier

description: Manual verification checklist for CodeMirror editor
steps: Verify: Can click file in explorer and see content in editor
Verify: Vim motions work (hjkl, w, b, $, 0, gg, G)
Verify: Vim modes work (i for INSERT, v for VISUAL, : for COMMAND, Esc for NORMAL)
Verify: Can edit content and see [+] in status bar
Verify: :w command saves file and removes [+]
Verify: Switching files when dirty shows unsaved changes dialog
Verify: Save/Discard/Cancel buttons work correctly
Verify: BottomDrawer is resizable with drag handle
Verify: Catppuccin Mocha theme matches design spec
Verify: Syntax highlighting works for .ts, .rs, .json files
